skill.name: market-research-reports
skill.description: Generate comprehensive market research reports with professional LaTeX formatting, strategic frameworks, and market visuals for business planning.
skill.summary: Create consulting-style market research reports with structured analysis and visuals.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high patterns, but most are false positives from LaTeX syntax, Markdown examples, and market sizing terms. The confirmed concern is operational: the visual generation helper invokes local Python tools and writes to a user-supplied output directory, so publication should include a filesystem warning.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output Directory Writes
medium_findings.description: The helper script accepts --output-dir, creates that directory, and writes generated images there. This is legitimate report generation behavior, but it can write outside the workspace if the user supplies an unsafe path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is clear and tied to user-controlled path input. Confidence is not higher because the action is expected for this reporting skill and does not show malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local External Tool Invocation
medium_findings.description: The helper script calls local scientific-schematics and generate-image Python scripts with subprocess.run. The command uses an argument array and no shell, so command injection is unlikely, but it executes additional local tooling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is confirmed, but the implementation avoids shell=True and uses fixed tool paths. The remaining risk is dependency behavior and filesystem side effects.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Mostly Documentation
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts point to Markdown code fences, inline LaTeX names, and example shell commands. These are documentation examples, not Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative lines show Markdown or LaTeX examples. I did not find Ruby backtick execution in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Static SAM Alerts Are Market Terminology
low_findings.description: Critical sensitive-data alerts for SAM refer to Serviceable Addressable Market in the TAM/SAM/SOM framework. No evidence found for Windows SAM database access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text defines market-sizing terms and generated visual labels. No Windows credential store paths or access behavior were found.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Alerts Are LaTeX Figure Paths
low_findings.description: The reported ../ sequences are hardcoded LaTeX figure references in templates. They are not used to read arbitrary user-supplied files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are template paths for figures and report assets. They are still worth noting because generated reports may include parent-directory image references.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: Searched the listed skill files for common prompt-injection phrases and claims of special authority. No evidence found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scan found no matching prompt-injection phrases in the reviewed files. Confidence is limited by semantic review scope, but no suspicious instruction block was observed.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Executes Local Visual Generators
dangerous_patterns.description: The script launches local Python visual generation helpers. It uses a list of arguments and a timeout, which reduces injection risk but still executes external code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The execution pattern is real and intentional. The command shape is constrained, but downstream helper scripts are outside this skill audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: User Path Controls Output Location
dangerous_patterns.description: The --output-dir option is converted directly to a Path and created. Users should keep outputs inside the project workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The output path flow is direct and easy to verify. It is a controlled user feature rather than evidence of malicious path traversal.

content.value_statement: Market teams need structured reports that combine data, visuals, and strategic frameworks. This skill creates consulting-style market research reports with LaTeX formatting and guided analysis.
actual_capabilities: Builds long-form market research reports with executive, market, competitor, risk, and recommendation sections.
Uses TAM, SAM, SOM, Porter Five Forces, PESTLE, SWOT, and BCG Matrix frameworks.
Provides LaTeX templates and a custom style package for professional report formatting.
Includes guidance for core charts, diagrams, infographics, and market analysis visuals.
Offers a Python helper for batch generation of standard market report visuals.
Documents report structure, data analysis patterns, and visual generation prompts.
limitations: It does not verify market data by itself; users must provide or gather reliable sources.
PDF output depends on a working LaTeX environment and required packages.
Visual generation depends on separate local skills or tools being installed.
The helper script can write files to paths supplied by the user.

use_cases.title: Client market entry report
use_cases.description: Prepare a structured market entry report with market size, competitors, risks, and recommended actions.

use_cases.title: Investment diligence brief
use_cases.description: Create a diligence report that explains growth, market structure, competitive forces, and investment risks.

use_cases.title: Opportunity assessment
use_cases.description: Analyze a product opportunity with customer segments, TAM/SAM/SOM, adoption drivers, and roadmap priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Start a market report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a market research report for [market]. Include scope, market size, growth drivers, competitors, risks, and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Add market sizing
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a TAM/SAM/SOM analysis for [market]. State assumptions, segments, source needs, and confidence levels.

prompt_templates.title: Create competitive analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Write the competitive landscape section for [market]. Include Porter Five Forces, key players, positioning, and strategic implications.

prompt_templates.title: Produce full deliverable
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a consulting-style market research report for [market] with LaTeX structure, core visuals, frameworks, risks, and an implementation roadmap.

output_examples.input: Create a report for the electric vehicle charging market.
output_examples.output: A structured market report with executive summary, market sizing, growth forecast, competitor mapping, risk heatmap, and recommendations.

output_examples.input: Analyze the AI in healthcare opportunity for a product team.
output_examples.output: An opportunity assessment with TAM/SAM/SOM, segment attractiveness, regulatory risks, adoption drivers, and roadmap priorities.

output_examples.input: Prepare a competitive landscape chapter for cloud security.
output_examples.output: A strategy chapter with Porter Five Forces, market share discussion, positioning matrix, competitor profiles, and strategic implications.

best_practices: Gather current market data and source notes before generating final report sections.
Keep generated files inside a project workspace and review output paths before running scripts.
State assumptions clearly for market sizing, forecasts, and strategic recommendations.
anti_patterns: Do not present estimates without sources, assumptions, or confidence levels.
Do not run visual generation scripts with broad system paths as output directories.
Do not rely on a single framework when the decision requires market, competitor, and risk views.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured market research reports with strategy frameworks, report templates, and visual planning guidance.

faq.question: Does it gather live market data automatically?
faq.answer: No. Users should gather current data separately and provide sources for the report.

faq.question: What formats does it support?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on LaTeX source that can be compiled into a professional PDF.

faq.question: Which frameworks are included?
faq.answer: It includes TAM/SAM/SOM, Porter Five Forces, PESTLE, SWOT, BCG Matrix, risk heatmaps, and opportunity matrices.

faq.question: Does it require other tools?
faq.answer: The templates are usable alone, but visual generation expects local scientific-schematics or generate-image tools.

faq.question: What security warning should users know?
faq.answer: The visual helper writes to a user-supplied output directory, so users should choose a safe project folder.
